contract_equivalence_conditions.condition: The verifier durably records receive id and payload hash before parsing, verification, or rejection.
contract_equivalence_conditions.implementation_hook: AttemptInbox insert or equivalent inbox key before host-code validation

contract_equivalence_conditions.condition: The report commitment and certificate fields are bound to an allowed attested execution identity.
contract_equivalence_conditions.implementation_hook: attestation verifier, PCR/algorithm allow-list, commitment recomputation

contract_equivalence_conditions.condition: An issued source token is conditionally consumed at most once for an accepted report.
contract_equivalence_conditions.implementation_hook: SourceToken compare/update or serializable row update

contract_equivalence_conditions.condition: Terminal, source, counter, and nonce keys are separately unique before acceptance.
contract_equivalence_conditions.implementation_hook: PK(rid,nonce), unique source, unique seller/session/counter, unique seller/session/nonce

contract_equivalence_conditions.condition: The accepted row joins the attested policy to an allowed price row before visibility.
contract_equivalence_conditions.implementation_hook: transaction-time PricePolicy join or equivalent conditional write

contract_equivalence_conditions.condition: Exact/private-exact/sealed disclosure state satisfies the selected market policy.
contract_equivalence_conditions.implementation_hook: exact budget check, confidential-verifier check, or class-map check

contract_equivalence_conditions.condition: Sealed-class rows include required deposit, audit, rejection, deadline, or optional rate-limit evidence.
Retries and crashes cannot expose partial accepted rows or duplicate queue entries.
contract_equivalence_conditions.implementation_hook: savepoints, idempotent rejection keys, consumed-missing recovery

contract_equivalence_conditions.condition: Estimators, payment joins, and buyer queries see only rows that passed every admission predicate.
contract_equivalence_conditions.implementation_hook: AggregateQueue insert after all checks or validated materialized view

contract_equivalence_conditions.condition: Every malformed durable receive or parsed deterministic admission failure has a queryable rejection reason and no queue entry.
contract_equivalence_conditions.implementation_hook: RejectLog row keyed by terminal key for parsed failures or receive id for malformed attempts

contract_equivalence_conditions.condition: Expired precommit or release attempts are counted for fill-rate and never enter aggregation.
contract_equivalence_conditions.implementation_hook: consumed_missing lifecycle state and fill-rate denominator

description: visible-state lifecycle invariants and contract-equivalence conditions for CertificateAdmit

invariants.condition: Every visible accepted, rejected, or consumed-missing outcome is linked to a durable receive record.
invariants.violation_query: abstract predicate: terminal or reject row has no prior AttemptInbox receive id

invariants.condition: Each source token appears in at most one accepted queue row.
invariants.violation_query: SELECT src FROM AggregateQueue GROUP BY src HAVING COUNT(*)>1;

invariants.condition: No two accepted rows share a terminal, counter, or nonce freshness key.
invariants.violation_query: GROUP BY rid,nonce OR seller,session,ctr OR seller,session,nonce HAVING COUNT(*)>1

invariants.condition: Every accepted row uses an allowed policy and price row checked at admission.
invariants.violation_query: SELECT accepted.* FROM accepted LEFT JOIN PricePolicy USING(policy,priceID) WHERE PricePolicy.priceID IS NULL;

invariants.condition: The accepted report commitment recomputes from the submitted report and certificate fields.
A deterministic rejection has a queryable reason and no aggregate queue row.
invariants.violation_query: SELECT rid,nonce FROM RejectLog INTERSECT SELECT rid,nonce FROM AggregateQueue;

invariants.condition: Expired precommit/released rows become consumed-missing and never enter the queue.
invariants.violation_query: SELECT rid,nonce FROM CertLedger WHERE status='consumed_missing' INTERSECT SELECT rid,nonce FROM AggregateQueue;

invariants.condition: Every aggregate queue row has an accepted certificate row with the same terminal key.
invariants.violation_query: SELECT q.rid,q.nonce FROM AggregateQueue q LEFT JOIN CertLedger c ON q.rid=c.rid AND q.nonce=c.nonce AND c.status='accepted' WHERE c.rid IS NULL;

invariants.condition: Exact/private-exact rows satisfy the realized-budget predicate; sealed rows satisfy class, price-row, and residual-evidence predicates.
invariants.covered_by: exact mismatch, class mismatch, residual mismatch tests
invariants.violation_query: abstract predicate over mode, q, realized budget, class map, residual evidence
